items.original: Меня опять терзают смутные сомнения… У Шпака — магнитофон, у посла — медальон
items.translation:
Vague doubts haunt me once again...
Shpak's tape recorder, the ambassador's medallion.
items.reference: Vague doubts haunt me once again... Shpak's tape recorder, the ambassador's medallion.
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. The mock-heroic phrasing is preserved, and the elliptical “У Шпака — …, у посла — …” structure is kept without incorrectly turning it into simple possession. The comedic callback and register clash work as intended.

items.original: Здравствуйте, царь, очень приятно. Царь, очень приятно, царь. Очень приятно, здравствуйте, царь.
items.translation: Hello, I’m the Tsar, very pleased to meet you. The Tsar, very pleased. Tsar, very pleased. Hello, the Tsar.
items.reference: Hello, I’m the Tsar, very pleased to meet you. The Tsar, very pleased. Tsar, very pleased. Hello, the Tsar.
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. The repetitive, banal Soviet-style politeness is preserved, maintaining the era-clash humor and Bunsha’s drunken, socially automatic self-introduction.

items.translation: I demand that the banquet continue!
items.reference: I demand that the banquet continue!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. The authoritarian, pompous demand is rendered directly and keeps the comic escalation of Bunsha’s pseudo-royal persona.

items.original: Оставь меня, старушка, я в печали
items.translation: Leave me, old woman, I am in sorrow.
items.reference: Leave me, old woman, I am in sorrow.
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. The archaic/high-register melancholy is preserved, and “old woman” keeps the comic insult/epoch mismatch in the modern setting.

items.translation: They've walled me in... Walled me in, the demons!
items.reference: They've walled me in... Walled me in, the demons!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. The subject assignment (“the demons” as agents) and the panicked repetition are preserved, keeping the joke of technological misunderstanding.

items.translation: Behold the power of the life-giving cross!
items.reference: Behold the power of the life-giving cross!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. The elevated, religiously emphatic register is maintained, supporting the humor of attributing a mechanical event to a miracle.

items.translation: The floor is open to everyone!
items.reference: The floor is open to everyone!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. The line functions as a punchy MC-style command that triggers mass dancing; the chosen English idiom preserves the performative effect.

items.translation: Now, this was a good call!
items.reference: Now, this was a good call!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. The breezy self-satisfaction and criminal opportunism are conveyed in a compact, idiomatic way suitable for the character’s tone.

items.original: Икра чёрная, красная… Да! Заморская икра… баклажанная!
items.translation: Black caviar, red caviar... and yes! Overseas spread... eggplant!
items.reference: Black caviar, red caviar... and yes! Overseas spread... eggplant!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. Crucially avoids calling the eggplant item “caviar” in English, preserving the Soviet-satire inversion and preventing confusion while keeping the comedic beat (“overseas… eggplant!”).

items.original: Три магнитофона, три кинокамеры, три портсигара отечественных, куртка… замшевая… Три
items.translation: Three tape recorders, three movie cameras, three cigarette cases, domestic... a suede jacket... Three.
items.reference: Three tape recorders, three movie cameras, three cigarette cases, domestic... a suede jacket... Three.
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. The comic rhythm of the inventory, the self-important “domestic,” and the trailing correction/afterthought structure are preserved.

items.original: И тебя вылечат… и тебя тоже вылечат… и меня вылечат…
items.translation: And they’ll cure you... and they’ll cure you too... and they’ll cure me...
items.reference: And they’ll cure you... and they’ll cure you too... and they’ll cure me...
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. The calm, sing-song fatalism is preserved, sustaining the darkly comic Soviet subtext about psychiatric “treatment” while keeping the escalating absurdity.

summary.interpretation: 0.00 MQM penalty points per meaning unit (≈ 0.00 major errors per unit; weights: minor=1, major=5, critical=10)
